info.title: CanYouGrab API
info.description:
Confidence-scored domain availability API. Fast DNS lookups with on-demand RDAP/WHOIS verification and smart caching.

## Pipeline

Each domain goes through up to 5 stages: **cache** (Valkey) → **bloom filter** (zone file) → **DNS** (Unbound) → **TOS coverage check** → **RDAP/WHOIS** (rust-whois). Registered domains resolve at cache or DNS and return in <100ms. Available domains proceed to RDAP/WHOIS for high-confidence verification (~200ms for RDAP, ~7s for legacy WHOIS).

## TOS Coverage

RDAP and WHOIS queries are only made to registry operators explicitly listed in our [Terms of Service](https://portal.canyougrab.it/terms). This covers 820+ TLDs including all major gTLDs (.com, .net, .org, .io, .co, .dev, .app, .xyz, etc.) and popular ccTLDs (.de, .uk, .ca, .fr, .nl, .au, .jp, etc.). For uncovered TLDs (mostly brand TLDs like .apple, .nike), results are DNS-only with medium confidence.

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer API key in the `Authorization` header. You must accept the [Terms of Service](https://portal.canyougrab.it/terms) before making API calls — unauthenticated or non-TOS-accepted requests return 403.

## Rate Limits

Rate limits depend on your plan (free: 30/min, basic: 300/min, pro: 1000/min, business: 3000/min). Exceeding limits returns HTTP 429.

post.description:
Check availability of up to 100 domains in a single request. Each result includes a confidence score, data source, cache freshness, and registration details when available.

The lookup pipeline: Valkey cache → bloom filter → DNS (Unbound) → TOS coverage gate → RDAP/WHOIS (rust-whois).

- **Registered domains**: Resolved at cache, bloom, or DNS — return in <100ms with high confidence.
- **Available domains (covered TLDs)**: Verified by RDAP (~200ms) or WHOIS (~7s) — return with high confidence.
- **Available domains (uncovered TLDs)**: DNS-only — return with medium confidence. RDAP/WHOIS is not queried for TLDs whose registry operator is not listed in our Terms of Service.

This is a long-poll endpoint: the connection stays open until all domains are resolved or a 45-second timeout is reached.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: When true, includes job-level timing metadata (job_id, queued_at, completed_at) in the response.

domains.description: Array of domain names to check (max 100, determined by your plan's domain cap)

200.description: Domain availability results with confidence scores

domain.description: The domain name that was checked

available.description: true = registrable, false = registered, null = could not determine

confidence.description: high = verified by RDAP/WHOIS or confirmed registered via DNS/bloom. medium = DNS-only (TLD not covered by TOS, or WHOIS unavailable). low = DNS failure or stale cache.

tld.description: Top-level domain extracted from the query

source.description: How this result was determined. cache = served from Valkey cache. bloom = zone file bloom filter (registered domains only). dns = Unbound NS/NXDOMAIN lookup. rdap = RDAP verification (fast, ~200ms). whois = legacy WHOIS verification (slower, ~7s). registry = TLD-level policy (brand/closed TLDs that don't allow public registration).

checked_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this data was gathered

cache_age_seconds.description: Seconds since this data was originally looked up. 0 = fresh lookup just now.

dns_status.description: Raw DNS lookup result. noerror_ns = NS records found (registered). nxdomain = domain does not exist (likely available). noanswer = no NS records but no error (treated as registered). timeout = DNS query timed out. Only present for fresh lookups, not cached results.

registration.description: Registration details from RDAP/WHOIS for taken domains. null when domain is available, when resolved via cache/bloom/DNS (no WHOIS data fetched), or when WHOIS data is unavailable.

error.description: Error code when available is null. Values: dns_servfail, dns_timeout, brand_tld (TLD does not allow public registrations, e.g. .nike, .apple, .google)

nameservers.description: Authoritative nameservers for the domain. Present for registered domains resolved via DNS or bloom filter. null for available domains and cache hits without NS data.

parked.description: true if the domain's nameservers match a known parking service (Sedo, Afternic, Bodis, HugeDomains, Dan.com, ParkingCrew, Above.com, Efty). false otherwise.

hosting_provider.description: Detected DNS/hosting provider based on nameserver patterns (e.g. 'Cloudflare', 'AWS Route 53', 'Google', 'Sedo'). null if nameservers don't match any known provider.

domain_age_days.description: Days since the domain was first registered. Derived from registration.created_at. null if registration data is unavailable.

expires_in_days.description: Days until domain expiration. Negative if already expired. Derived from registration.expires_at. null if registration data is unavailable.

mixed_results.summary: Typical response showing registered, available, parked, and brand TLD results

400.description: Invalid request (empty array or exceeds domain cap for your plan)

403.description: Terms of Service not accepted. You must accept the TOS at the portal before using the API.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of what the user needs to do

terms_url.description: URL to review the Terms of Service

portal_url.description: URL to sign in and accept the terms

required_version.description: The TOS version that must be accepted

accepted_version.description: The TOS version the user previously accepted, if any

detail.message: You must accept the Terms of Service before using the API. Please visit https://portal.canyougrab.it/terms to review the terms, then sign in at https://portal.canyougrab.it to accept them.

429.description: Rate limit or monthly quota exceeded

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying (for rate limits)

504.description: Processing timeout — results were not ready within 45 seconds. Retry the request.

example.message: Results were not ready within 45 seconds. Please retry.

get.description: Returns the current billing period usage summary for the authenticated API consumer, including lookups used, remaining quota, and plan details.

200.description: Usage summary for the authenticated consumer

monthly_limit.description: Total lookups allowed per billing period

lookups_used.description: Lookups consumed in the current billing period

lookups_remaining.description: Lookups remaining in the current billing period

period_start.description: Start of the current billing period

period_end.description: End of the current billing period

get.description: Returns API health status. No authentication required.

apiKey.description: API key passed as Bearer token in the Authorization header. Create keys at https://portal.canyougrab.it/api-keys
